message:
fix(ft-z5dxg): serialize CommandBuilder.envs via OsString→String lossy adapter + enable serde_support by default

The proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs suite was silently never running for two
independent reasons:

1. The `envs: BTreeMap<OsString, EnvEntry>` field cannot roundtrip through
   serde_json because `OsString` serializes as a non-string enum on unix
   (bytes variant) and JSON requires map keys to be strings. Any test that
   carried a non-trivial env map would fail at `serde_json::to_string`.
2. The `serde_support` feature was not in the pty crate's default feature
   set, so the test file (`#![cfg(feature = "serde_support")]`) compiled
   to an empty unit under `cargo test` and quietly skipped every assertion.

Fix:

* New `env_map_serde` module provides a `#[serde(with = ...)]` adapter
  that (de)serializes the map as `BTreeMap<String, EnvEntry>` via
  `OsString::to_string_lossy` + `OsString::from`. POSIX env-var names are
  ASCII-only and Windows env names survive UTF-16→UTF-8 roundtrips, so
  any lossy conversion already indicates a key the OS itself rejects.
* `default = ["serde_support"]` in frankenterm/pty/Cargo.toml so the
  derived `Serialize`/`Deserialize` impls and the proptest suite are
  actually built under default `cargo test`. All current consumers of
  `portable-pty` in this workspace already opt into `serde_support`
  explicitly, so the default change is a no-op for them.

The existing proptest file keeps its `#![cfg(feature = "serde_support")]`
gate, but the feature is now on by default so the tests run.
